title: ॥ श्री सावित्री देवी स्तोत्रम् ॥
devanagariText:
॥ श्रीगणेशाय नमः ॥
॥ अथ श्रीसावित्रीदेवी स्तोत्रम् ॥
॥ ब्रह्मदेव उवाच ॥

नारायणस्वरूपे च नारायणि सनातनि।
नारायणसमुद्भूते प्रसन्ना भव सुन्दरि॥१॥

तेजःस्वरूपे परमे परमानन्दरूपिणि।
द्विजातीनां जातिरूपे प्रसन्ना भव सुन्दरि॥२॥

नित्ये नित्यप्रिये देवि नित्यानन्दस्वरूपिणि।
सर्वमङ्गलरूपेण प्रसन्ना भव सुन्दरि॥३॥

सर्वस्वरूपे विप्राणां मन्त्रसारे परात्परे।
सुखदे मोक्षदे देवि प्रसन्ना भव सुन्दरि॥४॥

विप्रपापेध्मदाहाय ज्वलदग्निशिखोपमे।
ब्रह्मतेजःप्रदे देवि प्रसन्ना भव सुन्दरि॥५॥

कायेन मनसा वाचा यत्पापं कुरुते द्विजः।
तत्ते स्मरणमात्रेण भस्मीभूतं भविष्यति॥६॥

॥ इति श्रीब्रह्मवैवर्तमहापुराणे प्रकृतिखण्डे श्रीब्रह्मदेवकृतं श्रीसावित्रीदेवीस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Gaṇeśāya Namaḥ ||
|| Atha Shrī Sāvitrī Devī Stotram ||
|| Brahmadeva Uvācha ||

Nārāyaṇa-svarūpe cha Nārāyaṇi sanātani,
Nārāyaṇa-samudbhūte prasannā bhava sundari. ||1||

Tejaḥ-svarūpe parame paramānanda-rūpiṇi,
Dvijātīnāṃ jāti-rūpe prasannā bhava sundari. ||2||

Nitye nitya-priye devi nityānanda-svarūpiṇi,
Sarva-maṅgala-rūpeṇa prasannā bhava sundari. ||3||

Sarva-svarūpe viprāṇāṃ mantra-sāre parāt-pare,
Sukha-de mokṣa-de devi prasannā bhava sundari. ||4||

Vipra-pāpedhma-dāhāya jvalad-agni-śikhopame,
Brahma-tejaḥ-prade devi prasannā bhava sundari. ||5||

Kāyena manasā vāchā yat pāpaṃ kurute dvijaḥ,
Tat te smaraṇa-mātreṇa bhasmī-bhūtaṃ bhaviṣyati. ||6||

|| Iti Shrī Brahma-Vaivarta Mahāpurāṇe Prakṛti-Khaṇḍe Shrī Brahmadeva-kṛtaṃ Shrī Sāvitrī-Devī Stotraṃ Sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री सावित्री देवी स्तोत्रम् ब्रह्मवैवर्त महापुराण के प्रकृतिखण्ड के तेईसवें अध्याय (श्लोक ७९-८४) में स्वयं ब्रह्मदेव द्वारा देवी सावित्री की स्तुति में रचित एक छह श्लोकों का अति प्राचीन एवं प्रभावशाली स्तोत्र है। इसमें ब्रह्माजी देवी सावित्री को नारायण-स्वरूपा, सनातनी, नारायण से प्रकट हुई परम सुन्दरी के रूप में सम्बोधित करते हैं और उनसे प्रसन्न होने की प्रार्थना करते हैं।

प्रथम श्लोक में ब्रह्मा देवी को 'नारायणस्वरूपा' (नारायण के समान रूप वाली) कहकर पुकारते हैं। द्वितीय श्लोक में देवी को परम तेजस्वरूपा, परमानन्दरूपिणी और द्विजों की जाति का स्वरूप बताया गया है। तृतीय श्लोक में सावित्री को नित्या (शाश्वत), नित्य-आनन्दमयी एवं समस्त मंगलों का साकार रूप कहा गया है। चतुर्थ श्लोक में देवी को विप्रों के मन्त्रों के सार, परात्पर, सुख तथा मोक्ष देने वाली के रूप में स्तुत किया गया है। पंचम श्लोक में देवी की उपमा प्रज्वलित अग्नि की शिखा से दी गई है — जो साधक के पापरूपी ईंधन को भस्म करने वाली तथा ब्रह्मतेज प्रदान करने वाली हैं। छठे श्लोक में फलश्रुति है — जो भी पाप द्विज ने काया, मन अथवा वाणी से किया हो, देवी सावित्री के स्मरणमात्र से वह भस्म हो जाता है।

यह स्तोत्र विशेष रूप से वट सावित्री व्रत के अवसर पर पठनीय है। सौभाग्यवती स्त्रियाँ अपने पति की दीर्घायु एवं अखण्ड सौभाग्य के लिए, तथा सभी साधक पापमुक्ति, ब्रह्मतेज की प्राप्ति एवं मोक्ष की कामना से इस स्तोत्र का नित्य पाठ कर सकते हैं।
benefits: Traditionally recited during Vat Savitri Vrat for marital harmony and the blessing of akhanda saubhagya (everlasting auspiciousness) for married women
Invokes the grace of Devi Savitri for the longevity and well-being of one's husband, in keeping with Savitri's pativrata legacy
Traditionally recited for purification of sins committed through body, mind, or speech — verse 6 explicitly states that mere remembrance of the Devi reduces such sins to ashes
Invokes Brahma-tejas (the radiance of Brahman) and inner illumination, especially valued by students of the Vedas and seekers of higher knowledge
Traditionally associated with bestowal of sukha (worldly happiness) and moksha (liberation) per verse 4
Invokes the eternal auspiciousness (sarva-mangala) of the Devi, suitable as a daily morning prayer
Composed by Lord Brahma himself in praise of his consort Savitri, carrying the unique authority of a Devata-krita stotra
description: Savitri Devi Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री सावित्री देवी स्तोत्रम्, is a six-verse hymn composed by Lord Brahma in praise of his consort Goddess Savitri. Drawn from the Brahma Vaivarta Mahapurana — Prakriti Khanda, Adhyaya 23, verses 79–84 — this stotra is one of the canonical Devata-krita (god-composed) hymns dedicated to Savitri, who in the Brahma Vaivarta tradition is enumerated among the five Pancha-Prakriti devis along with Radha, Durga, Lakshmi, and Saraswati. Each of the first five verses ends with the refrain 'prasannā bhava sundari' — 'be pleased, O beautiful one' — and the sixth verse offers a powerful phalashruti: that mere remembrance of the Devi reduces to ashes any sin committed through body, mind, or speech. This stotra is especially recited during Vat Savitri Vrat (observed on Jyeshtha Amavasya in North India and Jyeshtha Purnima in Western India), when married women fast and worship the banyan (vat) tree for the long life and well-being of their husbands, invoking the legend of Savitri who won back her husband Satyavan from Yama. With six verses and a recitation time of approximately three minutes, it is suitable for both daily worship and special vrat occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Savitri Devi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Savitri Devi Stotram and which scripture is it from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Savitri Devi Stotram (श्री सावित्री देवी स्तोत्रम्) is a six-verse hymn composed by Lord Brahma himself in praise of his consort Goddess Savitri, preserved in the Brahma Vaivarta Mahapurana — Prakriti Khanda, Adhyaya 23, verses 79-84. The colophon explicitly states: Iti Shri Brahma-Vaivarta Mahapurane Prakriti-Khande Shri Brahmadeva-kritam Shri Savitri-Devi Stotram Sampurnam — 'Thus ends the Savitri Devi Stotram composed by Lord Brahma, in the Prakriti Khanda of the Brahma Vaivarta Mahapurana.' This is one of the canonical Devata-krita (god-composed) hymns to Savitri, who in the Brahma Vaivarta tradition is enumerated among the five Pancha-Prakriti devis along with Radha, Durga, Lakshmi and Saraswati. (Source: Brahma Vaivarta Purana, Prakriti Khanda, Adhyaya 23, verses 79-84.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Savitri Devi Stotram the same as the Savitri-Satyavan Vat Savitri Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: These are related but distinct elements of Savitri tradition. The Savitri Devi Stotram from the Brahma Vaivarta Purana is a six-verse hymn by Lord Brahma in praise of his consort Goddess Savitri (the divine Pancha-Prakriti devi). The Vat Savitri Vrat (observed on Jyeshtha Amavasya in North India and Jyeshtha Purnima in Western India) commemorates the legendary pativrata Savitri who, in the Mahabharata Vana Parva (Pativrata Mahatmya Parva), won back her husband Satyavan from Yama through her tapas-shakti and dharma-jnana. The Vat Savitri Vrat is associated with the human Savitri (daughter of King Ashvapati), while the Brahma-krita stotra is for the divine Goddess Savitri. However, both are recited during Vat Savitri Vrat by married women, since both Savitris share the same name and the same protective shakti for husbands. (Source: Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Mahabharata Vana Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the refrain 'prasannā bhava sundari' mean and what is its significance?
seoFaqs.answer: The refrain prasannā bhava sundari concludes the first five verses of the Savitri Devi Stotram. Prasannā — 'pleased, gracious'; bhava — 'be'; sundari — 'O beautiful one.' Together: 'Be pleased, O beautiful Goddess.' Lord Brahma uses this refrain to repeatedly invoke Savitri's grace through five distinct dimensions: V1 — Narayanasvarupa (one with Narayana's nature); V2 — Tejahsvarupa (essence of pure radiance) and the source of dvija-jati; V3 — Nitya, Nitya-priya, Nityananda-svarupa, Sarva-mangala-rupa; V4 — Sarva-svarupa of all viprya-mantras, Mantra-sara, Param-para, Sukhada, Mokshada; V5 — like a blazing flame that burns the kindling of the dvija's sins, Brahma-tejah-prada. Each verse builds the picture of Savitri as the supreme grace-bestowing Devi. (Source: Savitri Devi Stotram verses 1-5.)

seoFaqs.question: What does verse 6 of the Savitri Stotra promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 6 of the Savitri Devi Stotram offers an extraordinarily powerful phalashruti: Kayena manasa vacha yat papam kurute dvijah, Tat te smarana-matrena bhasmi-bhutam bhavishyati — 'Whatever sin a dvija (twice-born) commits through body (kayena), mind (manasa) or speech (vacha), the same becomes reduced to ashes (bhasmi-bhutam) merely by remembering you (te smarana-matrena), O Devi.' This verse establishes the Savitri Devi Stotram as a powerful papa-shamana (sin-burning) prayer. The metaphor in verse 5 of Savitri as a blazing flame (jvalad-agni-shikhopame) that burns the 'kindling of sins' (papedhma-daha) is fulfilled in verse 6's promise that mere smarana (remembrance) of the Devi reduces accumulated sins to ashes. (Source: Savitri Devi Stotram verse 6.)

seoFaqs.question: When and on what occasions should the Savitri Devi Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Savitri Devi Stotram is most powerfully recited on Vat Savitri Vrat (Jyeshtha Amavasya in North India and Jyeshtha Purnima in Western India) — when married women fast and worship the banyan (vat) tree for the long life and well-being of their husbands. It is also auspicious on Friday (Shukravar — Devi's day) and Monday (Somvar — for husband-protection), during the entire month of Jyeshtha, on Mahalakshmi Vrata days, on Brahma Jayanti, on Hartalika Teej (Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya — when Parvati won Shiva), on Karva Chauth, and as nityapaath by sumangali women, students of the Vedas (since Savitri is the source of all viprya-mantras), and seekers of papa-shamana through verse 6's promise. (Source: Vat Savitri Vrat paddhati; Brahma Vaivarta Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Savitri Devi Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional teachers attribute the following benefits to regular recitation of the Savitri Devi Stotram with shraddha: bestowal of akhanda saubhagya (everlasting marital auspiciousness) for sumangali women; longevity (ayushya) and well-being of one's husband, in keeping with Savitri's pativrata legacy; complete purification of sins committed through body, mind or speech (verse 6); awakening of Brahma-tejas (the radiance of Brahman-inquiry — verse 5); bestowal of both sukha (worldly happiness) and moksha (liberation — verse 4); the eternal grace (sarva-mangala) of the Devi as a daily morning prayer; and the unique authority of a Devata-krita stotra composed by Brahma himself, which carries weight beyond ordinary human-composed hymns. (Source: Savitri Devi Stotram phalashruti; Brahma Vaivarta tradition.)
